title: HOME FREE - 211info
text: HOME FREE HOME FREE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual intervention program that includes services such as: -Emergency Services: Hotline, information and referral, and telephone advocacy -Housing Services: Home visits, long-term advocacy and support, help to locate and maintain safe housing -Domestic and sexual violence healthcare and advocacy -Court and legal advocacy with a restraining order hotline -Economic Empowerment: Credit building and repair advocacy, savings, interview skills, employment seekers group, barrier reduction assistance, and Rent Well landlord-tenant education Motel vouchers are only available for those in emergencies who are actively fleeing domestic violence. Availability is dependent upon funding. Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Emergency Hotline: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Restraining Order Services: Monday-Friday 8am-2pm Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 235-8655 . Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 235-8655 Office [email protected] Hotline (503) 802-0506 Restraining Order Services [email protected] Hotline (503) 771-5503 Emergency Services [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information. No drop-in services available. Depending on call volumes, hotlines may not return calls or emails for 1-2 business days. If it is safe to receive a response, the caller should state it in their voicemail or email. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties (court advocacy in Multnomah County only) SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Advocacy * Domestic Violence Issues Case/Care Management * Domestic Violence Issues Credit Counseling * Domestic Violence Issues Domestic Violence Hotlines Domestic Violence Motel Vouchers Domestic Violence Support Groups Protective/Restraining Orders Specialized Information and Referral * Domestic Violence Issues Tenant Rights Information/Counseling * Domestic Violence Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
